law: Orthad remains exact, deterministic, non-scalar, and compiled from accrued QBL word only.
The old diagonal lens is retained as same-chart self-read/collapse, not discarded.
The canonical Orthad readout has two complementary orientation charts: point chart OmegaPlus and dual eigen-cochain chart OmegaMinus.
Cross-chart transfer between OmegaPlus and OmegaMinus is the off-diagonal readout location; off-diagonal terms are not inserted into one diagonal lens.
The finite cyclic successor sigma_N generated by QBL orientation history determines the dual cochain chart.
The reverse-transfer bilinear pairing has same-chart quadratic refinement q(r)=r^2/(2N), whose exponential is the native Gauss self-twist.
S/T symbol collision remains killed: use Weil_F_N and Weil_G_N for external targets; macro operators use non-colliding names.
A comparison to external modular notation may occur only after native transfer/self-twist objects are derived and sealed.
